A secondary bloodstream infection is a BSI that is seeded from a site-specific infection (pneumonia, NEC, or SSI) at another body site, except for catheter-associated infections. @tbl-secondary-bsi-rates shows the rate at which a secondary BSI is attributed to different primary infection types based on the NeoIPC criteria (organism match within a 17-day window around the primary infection). High rates of secondary BSI may indicate delayed diagnosis, inadequate source control, or particularly virulent pathogens, and warrant careful review of clinical protocols and infection management strategies.
